figures.caption: Fig. 1: Scenarios which benefit from incorporating the QCNet marginals prior. Regions of interest are marked in red. In both scenarios, not incorporating the prior leads to some modes violating environmental constraints (i.e., driving in the non-driveable area) and, also, decreased accuracy. With the prior introduced, ECTraj gives a more accurate prediction compared to OptTrajDiff, particularly in Scenario 1 (a).
Fig. 2: Discrete lognormal time step distribution for N = 20
